d: prescribed [M] without documenting the clinical diagnosis or indication on the prescription order, violating the Medication Dispensing Policy and CBAHI MM.5.3.3
r: Absence of mandatory diagnosis field enforcement combined with insufficient prescriber orientation on MOH dispensing documentation standards

d: prescribed [M] for a duration exceeding the 3-day supply limit for ER prescriptions, violating the ED Prescription Policy
r: Absence of system controls enforcing ER prescription duration limits and insufficient prescriber awareness of ER prescribing boundaries
ft: No duration limit on Rx form

d: prescribed [M] at an incorrect dose deviating from established dosing guidelines, posing risk of therapeutic failure or toxicity
r: Absence of automated dose-range checking combined with failure to consult dosing references and lack of independent dose verification

d: prescribed [M] which was identified as incorrect medication selection, potentially due to LASA drug name confusion, violating CBAHI MM.5
r: Inadequate therapeutic verification compounded by LASA confusion and absence of electronic prescribing with indication-to-drug matching

d: prescribed [M] via an incorrect route of administration inconsistent with the drug formulation, posing significant safety risk
r: Absence of route-formulation verification safeguards and insufficient prescriber knowledge of route-specific formulations

d: prescribed [M] with an incorrect dosing frequency deviating from pharmacokinetic dosing intervals, risking supra/subtherapeutic levels
r: Failure to verify correct dosing interval against drug monograph and absence of frequency validation in prescribing workflow

p: Medication Safety / Clinical Pharmacy Screening Protocol
d: prescribed [M] without assessing drug-drug interactions with existing regimen; a clinically significant interaction was identified during pharmacy screening
r: Inadequate medication reconciliation and interaction screening combined with absence of automated interaction checking
ft: No interaction database at point of care

d: prescribed [M] without verifying patient allergy status; patient had documented/reported allergy not cross-referenced, posing risk of allergic reaction or anaphylaxis
r: Failure to perform mandatory allergy verification combined with incomplete allergy documentation and absence of automated allergy cross-checking

d: prescribed [M], a high-alert medication, without adhering to mandatory protocols including independent double-check and enhanced monitoring per CBAHI MM.4
r: Failure to activate high-alert safety protocols due to insufficient awareness of classification and absence of forced-function safeguards

d: prescribed [M] which falls outside the prescriber's authorized scope of practice or prescribing privileges per institutional credentialing, violating CBAHI GLD.7
r: Failure to recognize privilege limitations combined with inadequate privilege verification in prescribing workflow and insufficient credentialing orientation

d: prescribed [M] which is contraindicated for the patient's condition (pregnancy, renal, hepatic or other), posing risk of organ toxicity or fetal harm
r: Failure to screen for contraindications combined with absence of automated alerts and insufficient assessment of patient risk factors

p: Medication Reconciliation and Therapeutic Duplication Policy
d: prescribed [M] resulting in therapeutic duplication with an existing medication from the same class, increasing toxicity risk
r: Inadequate medication reconciliation combined with absence of automated duplication checking and failure to review complete medication profile

p: Verbal and Telephone Order Policy (CBAHI MM.8)
d: issued a verbal/telephone order for [M] without read-back verification, timely authentication, or proper documentation per CBAHI MM.8
r: Non-compliance with verbal order safety protocols including read-back and timely co-signature, with insufficient enforcement of documentation requirements

d: prescribed [M], a controlled substance, without adhering to SFDA regulations, Raqeeb platform requirements, and institutional controlled substance protocols
r: Non-compliance with controlled substance regulations combined with insufficient familiarity with SFDA and Raqeeb requirements

d: prescribed [M] which is not in the approved formulary without obtaining PT&C non-formulary approval, violating CBAHI MM.6
r: Prescriber bypass of non-formulary approval combined with insufficient formulary familiarity and absence of automated formulary verification

d: issued a prescription for [M] missing mandatory elements (identifiers, dose, route, frequency, duration, or diagnosis) per MOH and CBAHI MM.7 standards
r: Failure to complete mandatory elements combined with inadequate form design and insufficient training on documentation standards

d: prescribed [M] for a pediatric patient without proper weight-based dose calculation, deviating from recommended mg/kg dosing
r: Failure to perform weight-based calculation combined with absence of pediatric dose calculators and insufficient pediatric prescribing training

d: resulted in dispensing of an incorrect medication instead of prescribed [M], potentially due to LASA confusion or similar packaging
r: Failure of dispensing verification including double-check and barcode scanning combined with LASA confusion and insufficient shelf separation

d: resulted in dispensing of incorrect strength of [M]; correct drug but wrong strength due to similar packaging or inadequate label verification
r: Inadequate strength verification compounded by similar packaging and absence of barcode verification

d: resulted in dispensing of incorrect dosage form of [M] (e.g. tablet vs liquid or IR vs SR), altering the pharmacokinetic profile
r: Failure to verify dosage form against prescription combined with similar naming and insufficient dispensing verification

d: resulted in dispensing of expired [M], violating CBAHI MM.13 and MM.14 storage standards; expired medications may have reduced efficacy
r: Inadequate expiry monitoring and FEFO rotation combined with failure to verify expiration during dispensing

d: resulted in dispensing of [M] with incorrect, incomplete, or missing labeling per CBAHI MM.12 requirements
r: Failure to apply complete labeling per CBAHI MM.12 combined with labeling system limitations and insufficient quality check

d: resulted in dispensing of incorrect quantity of [M]; quantity did not match prescription, potentially causing insufficient or excess supply
r: Failure to verify quantity against prescription combined with counting errors and insufficient independent verification

d: resulted in administration of [M] to the wrong patient due to failure in two-patient-identifier verification per CBAHI IPSG.1
r: Failure to execute two-identifier verification combined with absence of BCMA technology

d: resulted in administration of [M] outside the acceptable timing window (more than 30 min from scheduled time) without clinical justification
r: Inadequate administration scheduling combined with absence of eMAR timing alerts and high nursing workload

d: resulted in omission of a scheduled dose of [M] without documented justification; omission risks subtherapeutic levels and treatment failure
r: Absence of automated administration reminders combined with high workload and inadequate shift handoff communication

p: IV Medication Administration and Infusion Safety Policy
d: resulted in administration of [M] via IV at incorrect rate, posing risk of toxicity, hemodynamic instability, or subtherapeutic delivery
r: Failure to verify infusion rate against guidelines combined with absence of smart pump with DERS and insufficient IV competency

d: resulted in administration of [M] using incorrect technique (wrong site, improper reconstitution, or incorrect inhaler instruction), compromising bioavailability
r: Insufficient training on medication-specific techniques combined with inadequate competency verification and absence of standardized protocols

d: involved failure to initiate required TDM or monitoring for [M], increasing risk of undetected toxicity or therapeutic failure
r: Absence of automated monitoring prompts combined with insufficient prescriber awareness of medication-specific monitoring requirements

d: involved incomplete documentation in the MAR for [M], including missing administration records or inadequate reconciliation documentation
r: Inadequate documentation practices combined with workload pressure and absence of electronic documentation with mandatory fields

d: involved failure to identify and report an ADR associated with [M] through institutional and SFDA National Pharmacovigilance reporting systems
r: Insufficient ADR recognition and reporting awareness combined with inadequate pharmacovigilance training

d: involved storage of [M] under conditions deviating from manufacturer specifications, potentially compromising stability and efficacy
r: Inadequate storage monitoring combined with insufficient cold chain management and lack of automated temperature monitoring

d: involved a recalled or SFDA-withdrawn medication [M] not removed from inventory within the required timeframe per CBAHI MM.30
r: Failure of recall management to identify and remove products from all locations combined with inadequate notification communication
